reasoning: The ICE BofA US Corporate Index OAS recently reached 0.76%, an exceptionally tight level near historical floors. Investment-grade corporate credit spreads exhibit strong asymmetry: because they are bounded below (rarely falling below 0.70%-0.75%) but can widen significantly during periods of risk aversion or unexpected volatility, the probability of widening from this low base is higher than the probability of further tightening. While macroeconomic conditions (e.g., lower interest rates, soft landing) help keep spreads tight, the lack of further compression room combined with standard market volatility over a three-month horizon makes an increase in the spread more likely than a decrease. Therefore, I assign a 65% probability that the OAS will be higher by the implied resolution date (assumed to be in early 2026) than its value on 2025-11-09.
